titleStmt.title: SII 9.17: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 17. (A.R. No. 98 of 1913.) ON A PILLAR IN A FIELD TO THE EAST OF THE MALLIKARJUNA TEMPLE AT KAMBADURU, KALYANADRUG TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: This undated record refers itself to the reign of the Noḻamba-Pallava king Vīra-Nombādhirāja, who is stated to have bestowed on Ajjaparvara Bhidiyeṟeya a bracelet and called him Noḻambadoḍḍa, at the time of the construction of the Beddugonde tank. Another bracelet was bestowed on him by Śrīmu[kha*]rāma who called him Śrīmukadoḍḍa and a third bracelet was presented to him by Siṅgapōtarasa, Ponnēṟarasa and Dharmamahādēvī who called him Ponnēradoḍḍa. Noḻambadoḍḍa and others built a temple for the god Mahādēva. Since the inscription belongs palaeographically to the 9th century A.D. the king referred to here was probably Poḻalchōṟa-Nombādhirāja I.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti .samadhiga
tapañcamahāśabda
pallavānvaya śrīprithuvī
vallabha pallavakulati
laka śrīvīranombhādhirāja
rājaparamēśvara beddugo
ndeya keṟeya kaṭṭippandu
Ajjaparvarabhidiyeṟeya
ra noḻambadoḍḍanendu kaḍaga
miṭṭar śrīmukharāmar śrīmu kado
ḍḍarendu kaḍagamiṭṭar siṅga
pōtarasaru ponnēṟarasaru
dharmmamahādēviyaruḍitiḻdupo
nnēṟadoḍḍarendu kaḍagamiṭṭar

noḻambadoḍḍaru
virvvaḍicāgigaḷu
mahādēvara dēgula
maṁ māḍisiThe inscription is unfinished.
Digital edition of SII 9.17 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
